role: Chief Justice of Iran (1989–1999); chairman of the Assembly of Experts (2015–2016)
bio: Ayatollah Mohammad Yazdi was a senior hardline cleric who served as Chief Justice of Iran from 1989 to 1999, appointed by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei). He was elected to the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979, representing Kermanshah Province. A member of the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party), he later served multiple terms on the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) and in the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts), briefly chairing the latter from 2015 to 2016. As head of the [Judiciary](/orgs/judiciary-of-iran) during the 1990s, he oversaw a period of political repression including crackdowns on the press and reformist activists. He was known as one of the most conservative figures in the clerical establishment.
editorial_notes: Born 1931 in Yazd. Died 2 December 2020. Chief Justice 1989–1999. Guardian Council member for multiple terms. Brief chairman of Assembly of Experts for Leadership 2015–2016.
